{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Control Manager",
  "description": "X-Force Engineering Limited is an engineering and infrastructure solutions company delivering high-quality engineering services across Nigeria and beyond. The company combines technical expertise, innovation, and strong project management to provide reliable engineering solutions.

The company offers services in design, construction, installation, project management, consultancy, and maintenance across civil, structural, mechanical, and electrical engineering disciplines. This integrated approach enables X-Force Engineering to manage projects from concept to completion while maintaining high standards of safety, quality, and efficiency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Internal Control Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja
Job type: Full-time (Onsite)

Role Overview


	The Internal Control Manager will be responsible for designing, implementing, and monitoring internal control systems to safeguard company assets, ensure financial accuracy, and promote operational efficiency across all departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain a robust internal control framework across the organization.
	Conduct regular risk assessments to identify control weaknesses and recommend corrective actions.
	Monitor compliance with company policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Review and audit financial transactions, procurement processes, and project expenditures.
	Investigate irregularities, fraud, or policy breaches and report findings to management.
	Coordinate with external auditors and regulatory bodies as required.
	Prepare detailed internal audit reports with actionable recommendations.
	Ensure controls are in place for project cost management and contract administration.
	Train and sensitize staff on internal control policies and ethical standards.
	Track implementation of audit recommendations and report on compliance progress.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum of 8 years of experience in internal control, audit, or a similar role.
	ICAN certification or any relevant professional certification &mdash; MANDATORY.
	Construction or engineering industry experience &mdash; Strong Advantage.
	Strong knowledge of accounting standards, financial regulations, and risk management.
	Proficiency in accounting and ERP software.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, and report-writing skills.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and professional discretion.


Key Competencies:


	Risk &amp; Compliance Management
	Financial Auditing
	Attention to Detail
	Critical Thinking &amp; Problem Solving
	Communication &amp; Stakeholder Management
	Ethical Judgement


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@xforceengineeringlimited.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Internal Control Manager Job at X-Force Engineering Limited




        
    

        
        X-Force Engineering Limited is an engineering and infrastructure solutions company delivering high-quality engineering services across Nigeria and beyond. The company combines technical expertise, innovation, and strong project management to provide reliable engineering solutions.

The company offers services in design, construction, installation, project management, consultancy, and maintenance across civil, structural, mechanical, and electrical engineering disciplines. This integrated approach enables X-Force Engineering to manage projects from concept to completion while maintaining high standards of safety, quality, and efficiency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Manager
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abuja
Job type: Full-time (Onsite)

Role Overview


	The Internal Control Manager will be responsible for designing, implementing, and monitoring internal control systems to safeguard company assets, ensure financial accuracy, and promote operational efficiency across all departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain a robust internal control framework across the organization.
	Conduct regular risk assessments to identify control weaknesses and recommend corrective actions.
	Monitor compliance with company policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
	Review and audit financial transactions, procurement processes, and project expenditures.
	Investigate irregularities, fraud, or policy breaches and report findings to management.
	Coordinate with external auditors and regulatory bodies as required.
	Prepare detailed internal audit reports with actionable recommendations.
	Ensure controls are in place for project cost management and contract administration.
	Train and sensitize staff on internal control policies and ethical standards.
	Track implementation of audit recommendations and report on compliance progress.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Minimum of 8 years of experience in internal control, audit, or a similar role.
	ICAN certification or any relevant professional certification — MANDATORY.
	Construction or engineering industry experience — Strong Advantage.
	Strong knowledge of accounting standards, financial regulations, and risk management.
	Proficiency in accounting and ERP software.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, and report-writing skills.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and professional discretion.


Key Competencies:


	Risk & Compliance Management
	Financial Auditing
	Attention to Detail
	Critical Thinking & Problem Solving
	Communication & Stakeholder Management
	Ethical Judgement


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Receptionist / Bank Teller",
  "description": "Prospera, a&nbsp;Finance house, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist / Bank Teller
Job Location:&nbsp;Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a smart, courteous, and detail-oriented Receptionist/Bank Teller to serve as the first point of contact for clients while efficiently handling basic financial transactions.
	The ideal candidate will demonstrate excellent customer service skills, professionalism, and accuracy in handling cash and client requests.


Key Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to clients in a professional and friendly manner
	Handle cash deposits, withdrawals, and other basic financial transactions
	Maintain accurate records of all transactions
	Respond to customer inquiries and provide relevant information on products/services
	Ensure proper documentation and compliance with company policies
	Manage front desk activities including calls, emails, and visitor logs
	Escalate complex customer issues to the appropriate department
	Maintain a clean and organized reception area.


Requirements


	Age: 26 years and below
	0&ndash;2 years pf experience in a similar role (Banking experience is an added advantage)
	Must have completed NYSC
	Minimum of Second Class Lower (2:2) in any relevant discipline
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic knowledge of financial transactions and cash handling
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.


Preferred Skills:


	Customer service orientation
	Ability to work under pressure
	Good numerical and analytical skills
	Professional appearance and attitude.


Salary
N150,000 - N170,000 per month.&nbsp;",

Receptionist / Bank Teller Job at Prospera Finance




        
    

        
        Prospera, a Finance house, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Receptionist / Bank Teller
Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a smart, courteous, and detail-oriented Receptionist/Bank Teller to serve as the first point of contact for clients while efficiently handling basic financial transactions.
	The ideal candidate will demonstrate excellent customer service skills, professionalism, and accuracy in handling cash and client requests.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Welcome and attend to clients in a professional and friendly manner
	Handle cash deposits, withdrawals, and other basic financial transactions
	Maintain accurate records of all transactions
	Respond to customer inquiries and provide relevant information on products/services
	Ensure proper documentation and compliance with company policies
	Manage front desk activities including calls, emails, and visitor logs
	Escalate complex customer issues to the appropriate department
	Maintain a clean and organized reception area.


Requirements


	Age: 26 years and below
	0–2 years pf experience in a similar role (Banking experience is an added advantage)
	Must have completed NYSC
	Minimum of Second Class Lower (2:2) in any relevant discipline
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Basic knowledge of financial transactions and cash handling
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	High level of integrity, accuracy, and attention to detail.


Preferred Skills:


	Customer service orientation
	Ability to work under pressure
	Good numerical and analytical skills
	Professional appearance and attitude.


Salary
N150,000 - N170,000 per month.     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Junior Architect",
  "description": "Eudora &amp; Aegle Management Consulting - Our client is recruiting suiitably&nbsp;qualified canddiates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Architect

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Eudora &amp; Aegle Management Consulting, on behalf of its client is recruiting for the position ofa Junior Architect to bridge the gap between design and execution.
	You will be the primary technical representative for the firm during construction phases, ensuring that high-level design intent is translated accurately into physical structures.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct regular site visits to monitor progress and ensure work aligns with approved architectural plans.
	Document site activities, take progress photographs, and prepare weekly site reports for the Senior Architect.
	Identify discrepancies between drawings and physical construction early and propose technical solutions.
	Liaise with contractors, site foremen, and MEP consultants to clarify design queries.
	Assist in producing and refining detailed construction drawings (plans, sections, and elevations).
	Update &quot;As-Built&quot; drawings as the project evolves on-site.
	Assist in the selection and sourcing of finishes, fixtures, and construction materials.
	Represent the firm in site meetings and record minutes for review.
	Ensure that all site-related permits and safety protocols are being followed.
	Manage project files, ensuring the most recent versions of drawings are available to the site team.


Required Qualifications &amp; Skills


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in architecture.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in designing and site management.
	Must possess professional certification(s) in the said role.
	Must be willing and ready to travel.
	Advanced skills in Revit, SketchUp, AutoCAD, Lumion or other specialized Architectural software.
	Expert ability to interpret complex architectural, structural, and MEP drawings to ensure precise on-site execution and proactive resolution of construction conflicts.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter&nbsp;to:&nbsp;hr@eudoraandaeglegroup.com, CC.&nbsp;f.nwokoma@eudoraandaeglegroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Junior Architect Job at Eudora & Aegle Group




        
    

        
        Eudora & Aegle Management Consulting - Our client is recruiting suiitably qualified canddiates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Architect

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Eudora & Aegle Management Consulting, on behalf of its client is recruiting for the position ofa Junior Architect to bridge the gap between design and execution.
	You will be the primary technical representative for the firm during construction phases, ensuring that high-level design intent is translated accurately into physical structures.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Conduct regular site visits to monitor progress and ensure work aligns with approved architectural plans.
	Document site activities, take progress photographs, and prepare weekly site reports for the Senior Architect.
	Identify discrepancies between drawings and physical construction early and propose technical solutions.
	Liaise with contractors, site foremen, and MEP consultants to clarify design queries.
	Assist in producing and refining detailed construction drawings (plans, sections, and elevations).
	Update "As-Built" drawings as the project evolves on-site.
	Assist in the selection and sourcing of finishes, fixtures, and construction materials.
	Represent the firm in site meetings and record minutes for review.
	Ensure that all site-related permits and safety protocols are being followed.
	Manage project files, ensuring the most recent versions of drawings are available to the site team.


Required Qualifications & Skills


	Bachelor’s degree in architecture.
	1–2 years of experience in designing and site management.
	Must possess professional certification(s) in the said role.
	Must be willing and ready to travel.
	Advanced skills in Revit, SketchUp, AutoCAD, Lumion or other specialized Architectural software.
	Expert ability to interpret complex architectural, structural, and MEP drawings to ensure precise on-site execution and proactive resolution of construction conflicts.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Investment Manager",
  "description": "Elevated HR Limited is a diversified one stop place for employers looking to recruit talents to fill the different positions within their organization.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Investment Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for an experienced and hard-working individual with experience in investment banking and deposit mobilization to join our client Micro-Finance Bank.
	In this role, you will be charge with the core responsibilies of Investment management, deposit mobilization and fostering of excellent relationship between clients and the Bank.


Job Responsibilities


	Drive investment mobilization by sourcing, managing, and closing high-value individual and corporate clients.
	Lead, supervise, and coordinate the investment team to ensure individual and collective targets are achieved.
	Oversee client investment portfolios to ensure accuracy, profitability, and timely execution.
	Support the team in negotiations, deal structuring, and closing of complex investment transactions.
	Train, mentor, and continuously develop team members on investment products, sales strategies, and customer engagement.
	Ensure proper client onboarding, documentation, and adherence to internal investment procedures.
	Manage investment rollovers, maturities, renewals, and liquidation requests in line with agreed terms.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with investors while handling escalations and resolving client concerns.
	Ensure all investment activities comply with company policies, regulatory requirements, and risk controls.
	Prepare, analyze, and submit periodic reports on team performance, inflows, portfolios, and overall investment growth.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting/Finance, Banking and Economics or in any related field. Master&#39;s Degree would be additional advantage.
	3 &ndash; 5 + years in Investment Banking, Deposit mobilization and or Portfolio Management.
	Great Leadership and organizational skills.
	Working knowledge of Microsoft-suites.
	Business and planning skills.
	Market and commercial awareness.
	Demonstrative skills in Communication, negotiation and Presentation.


Salary
N500,000 - N700,000 / Month Plus commission

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;elevatedhumanresources@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Investment Manager&rsquo;&rsquo; as the subject of the email.",

Investment Manager Job at Elevated HR Limited




        
    

        
        Elevated HR Limited is a diversified one stop place for employers looking to recruit talents to fill the different positions within their organization.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Investment Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an experienced and hard-working individual with experience in investment banking and deposit mobilization to join our client Micro-Finance Bank.
	In this role, you will be charge with the core responsibilies of Investment management, deposit mobilization and fostering of excellent relationship between clients and the Bank.


Job Responsibilities


	Drive investment mobilization by sourcing, managing, and closing high-value individual and corporate clients.
	Lead, supervise, and coordinate the investment team to ensure individual and collective targets are achieved.
	Oversee client investment portfolios to ensure accuracy, profitability, and timely execution.
	Support the team in negotiations, deal structuring, and closing of complex investment transactions.
	Train, mentor, and continuously develop team members on investment products, sales strategies, and customer engagement.
	Ensure proper client onboarding, documentation, and adherence to internal investment procedures.
	Manage investment rollovers, maturities, renewals, and liquidation requests in line with agreed terms.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with investors while handling escalations and resolving client concerns.
	Ensure all investment activities comply with company policies, regulatory requirements, and risk controls.
	Prepare, analyze, and submit periodic reports on team performance, inflows, portfolios, and overall investment growth.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting/Finance, Banking and Economics or in any related field. Master's Degree would be additional advantage.
	3 – 5 + years in Investment Banking, Deposit mobilization and or Portfolio Management.
	Great Leadership and organizational skills.
	Working knowledge of Microsoft-suites.
	Business and planning skills.
	Market and commercial awareness.
	Demonstrative skills in Communication, negotiation and Presentation.


Salary
N500,000 - N700,000 / Month Plus commission

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using "Investment Manager’’ as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Generalist",
  "description": "Karbak Ventures Ltd. (ISO NG19/90729) is an Engineering, Procurement and Construction Company, with approximately two decades&rsquo; experience in Electro- Mechanical Projects for FMCG companies in West and East Africa. We have vast experience with plant/equipment installation of numerous sizes and complexity and we pride ourself in the vast capacity built over the years in providing design, procurement and installation supports to numerous FMCG firms such as Coca Cola, Nigeria Breweries, Guinness, Friesland, PZ Wilmar, Murzah Wilmar (Tanzania), Kasapreko (Ghana), etc. and supports for OEMS such Sidel, KHS, Krones, Pentair etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Generalist

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
Recruitment and Staffing:


	Manage full-cycle recruitment for diverse roles across engineering, procurement, and construction departments
	Develop job descriptions and post openings on appropriate platforms
	Screen resumes, conduct initial interviews, and coordinate with department managers for final selection
	Implement strategies to attract top talent in competitive technical fields


Employee Relations and Engagement:


	Handle employee inquiries, concerns, and grievances professionally and confidentially
	Promote a positive work environment through employee engagement initiatives
	Conduct exit interviews and provide insights to management for improving retention
	Mediate conflicts between employees or between employees and management
	Organize team-building activities and recognition programs


Compensation and Benefits Administration:


	Administer and explain employee benefits programs, including health insurance.
	Conduct salary surveys and assist in developing competitive compensation structures
	Process payroll changes related to new hires, terminations, and status changes
	Manage annual benefits enrollment process and serve as a liaison with benefit providers


Policy Development and Compliance:


	Develop, implement, and update HR policies and procedures aligned with company goals and labor laws
	Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations

Human Resource Generalist Job at Karbak Ven Limited




        
    

        
        Karbak Ventures Ltd. (ISO NG19/90729) is an Engineering, Procurement and Construction Company, with approximately two decades’ experience in Electro- Mechanical Projects for FMCG companies in West and East Africa. We have vast experience with plant/equipment installation of numerous sizes and complexity and we pride ourself in the vast capacity built over the years in providing design, procurement and installation supports to numerous FMCG firms such as Coca Cola, Nigeria Breweries, Guinness, Friesland, PZ Wilmar, Murzah Wilmar (Tanzania), Kasapreko (Ghana), etc. and supports for OEMS such Sidel, KHS, Krones, Pentair etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Generalist
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
Recruitment and Staffing:


	Manage full-cycle recruitment for diverse roles across engineering, procurement, and construction departments
	Develop job descriptions and post openings on appropriate platforms
	Screen resumes, conduct initial interviews, and coordinate with department managers for final selection
	Implement strategies to attract top talent in competitive technical fields


Employee Relations and Engagement:


	Handle employee inquiries, concerns, and grievances professionally and confidentially
	Promote a positive work environment through employee engagement initiatives
	Conduct exit interviews and provide insights to management for improving retention
	Mediate conflicts between employees or between employees and management
	Organize team-building activities and recognition programs


Compensation and Benefits Administration:


	Administer and explain employee benefits programs, including health insurance.
	Conduct salary surveys and assist in developing competitive compensation structures
	Process payroll changes related to new hires, terminations, and status changes
	Manage annual benefits enrollment process and serve as a liaison with benefit providers


Policy Development and Compliance:


	Develop, implement, and update HR policies and procedures aligned with company goals and labor laws
	Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations
	Stay informed about industry-specific regulations affecting the industry
	Conduct internal audits to ensure HR practices meet legal and company standards


Performance Management and Training:


	Coordinate and support the annual performance review process
	Assist managers in setting performance goals and developing improvement plans
	Identify training needs and coordinate or conduct training sessions
	Support succession planning initiatives for key positions


Onboarding and Offboarding:


	Conduct new employee orientations, ensuring smooth integration into the company
	Prepare and process new hire documentation
	Manage the offboarding process for departing employees, including exit interviews and benefits termination


HRIS and Reporting:


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date personnel records in the HRIS
	Generate HR reports and analytics to support decision-making
	Ensure data integrity and confidentiality of employee information


Project-Based HR Support:


	Collaborate with project managers to forecast and fulfill staffing needs for specific projects
	Manage contingent workforce, including contractors and temporary staff
	Assist in mobilizing employees for international assignments, if applicable


Workplace Safety and Wellness:


	Coordinate with the Safety department to ensure compliance with safety regulations
	Promote workplace wellness programs and initiatives
	Assist in managing workers' compensation claims and return-to-work programs


Strategic HR Initiatives:


	Contribute to long-term workforce planning and talent management strategies
	Participate in HR budget planning and cost optimization efforts
	Support change management initiatives during organizational transitions.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.
